title: Orchis sambucina lusus incarnata

Orchis sambucina is a species of orchid in the genus Orchis, and it belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The classification of Orchis sambucina lusus incarnata reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other orchid species.

intro: Orchis sambucina lusus incarnata, also known as the incarnate green orchid, is a rare and lesser-known variant of the Orchis sambucina species, a type of wild orchid found in the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Orchis sambucina lusus incarnata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions and decoctions, which are often used to relieve coughs and congestion, as well as to aid in the recovery from respiratory infections.
